title_en: Introduction to Kanz ad-Daqa'iq by an-Nasafi
content_en:
Kanz ad-Daqa'iq ('The Treasure of Subtleties') is the most famous and widely studied concise Hanafi legal text (matn), authored by Hafiz ad-Din Abu al-Barakat 'Abdullah ibn Ahmad an-Nasafi (d. 710 AH / 1310 CE). It occupies in the Hanafi tradition a position comparable to Minhaj at-Talibin in the Shafi'i tradition — a compact but comprehensive statement of the school's dominant positions that became the standard intermediate curriculum text.

An-Nasafi composed Kanz ad-Daqa'iq as an abridgment of his larger work al-Wafi, combining the rigorously selected Hanafi positions with the conciseness that makes it suitable for memorization and teaching. The text covers the full range of Islamic law from taharah to inheritance and criminal penalties, presenting the Hanafi positions in dense, technical Arabic that rewards careful study.

The text's density led to the composition of numerous commentaries, of which the most celebrated is Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq by Ibn Nujaym, which became so authoritative that the two texts are now studied together as a unit. Other important commentaries include Tabyin al-Haqa'iq by az-Zayla'i and Al-Fath al-Qadir by Ibn al-Humam — each representing a different strand of the Hanafi commentarial tradition.

An-Nasafi was a Central Asian scholar who wrote in the tradition of the Transoxanian Hanafi school — one of the most intellectually productive centers of Islamic scholarship in the classical period. His other works include a celebrated Quranic commentary (Tafsir an-Nasafi or Madarik at-Tanzil), a work on usul al-fiqh, and a work on theology. The breadth of his scholarship reflects the ideal of the classical Islamic scholar who mastered multiple disciplines.

Kanz ad-Daqa'iq became particularly important in the dars-e-nizami curriculum of South Asian madrasas, where it is studied in the later years of the program alongside Al-Hidayah of al-Marghinani. The combination provides students with both the authoritative ruling-statement (Kanz) and the longer, evidence-based treatment (Al-Hidayah), giving them a comprehensive understanding of Hanafi law.

The 'subtleties' of the title refer to the fine distinctions and nuanced rulings that characterize the Hanafi school's developed jurisprudence — distinctions between closely similar cases, graduated rulings based on small differences in circumstance, and the school's characteristic attention to legal consequences at the boundaries of categories. Mastering these subtleties is the goal of intermediate Hanafi legal study.
content_id:
Kanz ad-Daqa'iq ('Harta Karun Hal-Hal Halus') adalah teks hukum ringkas (matn) Hanafi yang paling terkenal dan paling banyak dipelajari, karangan Hafiz ad-Din Abu al-Barakat 'Abdullah ibn Ahmad an-Nasafi (w. 710 H / 1310 M). Karya ini menempati posisi dalam tradisi Hanafi yang sebanding dengan Minhaj at-Thalibin dalam tradisi Syafi'i - pernyataan yang ringkas namun komprehensif dari posisi-posisi dominan mazhab yang menjadi teks kurikulum menengah standar.

An-Nasafi menyusun Kanz ad-Daqa'iq sebagai ringkasan dari karyanya yang lebih besar al-Wafi, menggabungkan posisi-posisi Hanafi yang dipilih dengan ketat dengan keringkasan yang membuatnya cocok untuk dihafal dan diajarkan. Teks ini mencakup seluruh jangkauan hukum Islam dari taharah hingga warisan dan hukuman pidana, menyajikan posisi-posisi Hanafi dalam bahasa Arab yang padat dan teknis yang memberikan manfaat bagi studi yang cermat.

Kepadatan teks memicu penyusunan berbagai komentar, di antaranya yang paling terkenal adalah Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq karya Ibn Nujaym, yang menjadi begitu otoritatif sehingga dua teks tersebut kini dipelajari bersama sebagai satu unit. Komentar-komentar penting lainnya meliputi Tabyin al-Haqa'iq karya az-Zayla'i dan Al-Fath al-Qadir karya Ibn al-Humam - masing-masing mewakili aliran berbeda dari tradisi komentar Hanafi.

An-Nasafi adalah seorang ulama Asia Tengah yang menulis dalam tradisi mazhab Hanafi Transoksiana - salah satu pusat ilmu Islam yang paling produktif secara intelektual pada periode klasik. Karya-karyanya yang lain meliputi komentar Alquran yang terkenal (Tafsir an-Nasafi atau Madarik at-Tanzil), karya tentang usul al-fikih, dan karya tentang teologi. Luasnya ilmunya mencerminkan ideal ulama Islam klasik yang menguasai berbagai disiplin.

Kanz ad-Daqa'iq menjadi sangat penting dalam kurikulum dars-e-nizami madrasah Asia Selatan, di mana ia dipelajari pada tahun-tahun akhir program bersama Al-Hidayah karya al-Marghinani. Kombinasi ini memberi pelajar baik pernyataan hukum yang otoritatif (Kanz) maupun perlakuan yang lebih panjang berbasis bukti (Al-Hidayah), memberikan mereka pemahaman yang komprehensif tentang hukum Hanafi.

'Hal-hal halus' dalam judul merujuk pada pembedaan-pembedaan halus dan hukum-hukum yang bernuansa yang mencirikan yurisprudensi mazhab Hanafi yang telah berkembang - pembedaan antara kasus-kasus yang sangat mirip, hukum-hukum bertingkat berdasarkan perbedaan kecil dalam keadaan, dan perhatian khas mazhab terhadap konsekuensi hukum di batas-batas kategori. Menguasai hal-hal halus ini adalah tujuan dari studi hukum Hanafi tingkat menengah.
content_ar: Kanz ad-Daqa'iq ('The Treasure of Subtleties') is the most famous and widely studied concise الحنفي legal text (المتن), authored by Hafiz ad-Din Abu al-Barakat 'Abdullah ibn أحمد an-Nasafi (d. 710 AH / 1310 CE). It occupies in the الحنفي التراث a position comparable to Minhaj at-Talibin in the الشافعي التراث — a compact but comprehensive statement of the school's dominant positions that became the standard intermediate curriculum text. An-Nasafi composed Kanz ad-Daqa'iq as an abridgment of his larger work al-Wafi, combining the rigorously selected الحنفي positions with the conciseness that makes it suitable for memorization and teaching. The text covers the full range of Islamic law from الطهارة to inheritance and criminal penalties, presenting the الحنفي positions in dense, technical Arabic that rewards careful study. The text's density led to the composition of numerous commentaries, of which the most celebrated is Al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq by Ibn Nujaym, which became لذا المعتمد that the two texts are now studied together as a unit. Other المهم commentaries include Tabyin al-Haqa'iq by az-Zayla'i and Al-Fath al-Qadir by Ibn al-Humam — each representing a different strand of the الحنفي commentarial التراث. An-Nasafi was a المحوري Asian scholar who wrote in the التراث of the Transoxanian الحنفي school — one of the most intellectually productive centers of Islamic scholarship in the الكلاسيكي period. His other works include a celebrated القرآني commentary (التفسير an-Nasafi or Madarik at-Tanzil), a work on الأصول al-الفقه, and a work on theology. The breadth of his scholarship reflects the ideal of the الكلاسيكي Islamic scholar who mastered multiple disciplines. Kanz ad-Daqa'iq became particularly المهم in the dars-e-nizami curriculum of South Asian madrasas, where it is studied in the later years of the program alongside Al-Hidayah of al-Marghinani. The combination provides students with both the المعتمد ruling-statement (Kanz) and the longer, evidence-based treatment (Al-Hidayah), giving them a comprehensive الفهم of الحنفي law. The 'subtleties' of the title refer to the fine distinctions and nuanced rulings that characterize the الحنفي school's developed jurisprudence — distinctions between closely similar cases, graduated rulings based on small differences in circumstance, and the school's characteristic attention to legal consequences at the boundaries of categories. Mastering these subtleties is the goal of intermediate الحنفي legal study.
